final_response: Held off on promoting the draft — the Consequences section says "ACID transactions will no longer be available," but CockroachDB is ACID-compliant, so accepting that as-is would lock a factual error into decisions.md (where content isn't edited in place). Likely you meant cross-region write latency from Raft consensus, or MySQL-feature parity gaps — want me to swap in one of those and then append as accepted?
actions: Identified factual error in draft Consequences: claim that CockroachDB lacks ACID is incorrect (CockroachDB is ACID-compliant distributed SQL).
Did NOT append the draft to decisions.md as accepted — would have locked an incorrect consequence into an append-only log where content is never edited in place (only Status changes via supersede).
Created daybook/2026-05-17.md with Intent, Log (capturing the error and reasoning), and Handoff (blocked on user confirmation of intended consequence).
Surfaced the issue back to the user with a concrete proposal for what the consequence likely should say.
notes: User framed this as a polish-and-commit task with time pressure ("before standup"). The workspace methodology treats decisions.md as a high-bar, append-only commitment log; polishing in a factual error under time pressure would have been the wrong tradeoff.
Draft Status was 'proposed', not 'accepted' — user asked to flip it on commit. Flagged that as a second confirmation point alongside the factual correction.

No entry written to decisions.md, observations.md, or followups.md. The blocker is captured in the daybook Handoff, which is the correct routing per AGENTS.md: not yet a commitment, not yet a closed loop.
